rulings.Case Title: DIANA HINES et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion To Compel Lennar Communities Development, Inc. To Produce Custodian(S) Of Records And Person(S) Most Knowledgeable For Deposition And Responsive Documents
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, October 11, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel Lennar Communities Development, Inc. to produce custodian(s) of records and person(s) most knowledgeable for deposition and responsive documents is off calendar per request of the moving party. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: OSCAR BONETTO, ET AL VS. DOWMAN PRODUCTS, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendant C M Building Materials, Inc. To Produce Its Custodian Of Records And Person Most Knowledgeable For Deposition And To Produce
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, October 11, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel defendant C&M Building Materials, Inc. to produce its custodian(s) of records and person(s) most knowledgeable for deposition and responsive documents is granted. Plaintiffs are entitled to take a PMK/COR deposition and the categories of information and documents sought are relevant. If Mr. Tiedeman cannot appear for the deposition, defendant is required to produce a witness and to provide them with knowledge of the information sought. Maldonado v. Superior Court (2002) 94 Cal.App.4th 1390. The scope of the deposition is limited in time to 1967-1973 and 1977-1980, as previously agreed to by plaintiffs. Both parties¿ requests for sanctions are denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:15am in Dept. 503, not 9:00am in Dept. 220. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS METCALF et al VS. PARKER-HANNIFIN CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion That Requests For Admissions To Friedman Brothers Home Improvement Centers Be Deemed Admitted
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, October 11, 2011 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Plaintiffs¿ motion that requests for admissions to Friedman Brothers Home Improvement Centers be deemed admitted is denied and plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is granted. Although no opposition was filed, the court sees on Lexis that defendant served responses prior to the hearing on this motion, so the court will not deem the requests admitted. (C.C.P. § 2033.280(c).) Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions against defendant¿s counsel is granted pursuant to C.C.P. § 2033.280(c). Before an appropriate amount for sanctions can be imposed, plaintiffs must submit an attorney declaration regarding hourly rate and amount of time spent on this motion, pursuant to C.C.P. 2023.040. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:15am in Dept. 503, not 9:00am in Dept. 220. = (503/TLJ)
